opts: Keep backing up until they shoot or pass — give them as much space as they need to make a choice
Close the gap slightly — you've given them enough space. Force them to make a play.
Turn and race toward your own net and let the goalie read the play from there
Poke check from right where you are — your stick is long enough to reach from a distance
Rush straight at the puck carrier and take the body before they can make their next pass
Take your side, force the carrier wide, and trust your partner to take the middle player
Back all the way to the crease and let the goalie read the shooter from up close
Chase the trailing forward behind the play so they can't receive a drop pass from behind
Give up the chase and skate calmly back so you're set for the next rush after the scoring chance
Get your stick into their lane — angle them toward the boards and get your body in front of the shooting lane
Try to grab them
Dive forward and slide on your belly to block the shot — your body can take away the whole lane
Keep shooting from the point over and over — shots from outside will eventually find their way through
Pull the puck back out of the zone, reset, and come in with a new entry that attacks a different spot
Dump the puck deep in the corner every time and send all five skaters to chase it down
Send every player straight at the net at once to crash the crease and overwhelm the goalie
Shoot to the right side as planned — the coach drew it up this way so stick to the plan
Read and react — pass to your breaking teammate. The plan changes when the situation changes.
Slow down, circle back to the blue line, and think about what the best option might be
Dump it into the corner and send your linemates in to regroup and start the cycle
Attack immediately — try to score another goal so the lead becomes safer with only 90 seconds left
Control the puck along the boards, protect it, and force the other team to come get it — use every second
Dump the puck deep into the other team's zone and forecheck hard to keep it pinned down there
Shoot it the length of the ice
Standing still with your stick on the ice so the passer has an easy target to aim for
Looking up and deciding where you're going with the puck before it arrives — pre-reading the play
Calling loudly for the puck so the passer knows exactly where you are on the ice
Watching the puck all the way onto your blade so you don't fumble the reception
Chase both attackers hard and try to attack and score on the rush the other way
One forward pressures the puck, the others recover their positions — don't panic
All five players retreat to the front of your own net
Ignore the rush and stay in the offensive zone ready for the next chance
Leave your spot every time the puck moves so you can keep pressure on whoever has it
When a player with the puck is in a dangerous spot — near the slot or about to pass to the slot
Never leave your spot — stay planted at your position
Leave only when your D partner calls for help on the puck
Follow the center all the way back toward your own zone to stay matched up
Hold the blue line but cheat back slightly — be ready if the puck turns over
Ignore them completely and focus on keeping the puck in deep
Call for the puck yourself so you can take the shot from the point
Say nothing and let your D handle it
Call out immediately — 'Two coming!' — to warn your D that forwards are breaking behind them
Wait a few seconds to see if your D notice
Yell for a line change so a fresh group can come on and sort the coverage out
Skate all the way out to the blue line so you cut their shooting angle down to almost nothing
Come to the top of the crease, stay on your feet as long as possible, and force them to make the first move
Drop to the butterfly as soon as they cross the blue line
Stay planted right on your goal line
Holding the puck as long as possible just to keep it away from the other team
A gap or opening — a shot lane, a pass to the slot, or a player breaking to the net
Confusion by passing the puck around as quickly as they can
A penalty call by trying to draw contact
Shoot the puck immediately before the forwards close the lane on you — any shot is a chance
Fake the shot to freeze them, then pass to the half-wall player who opened up when they committed to you
Hold onto the puck and wait for a cleaner shooting lane to open up on its own
Pass it back to the other point
Both skate straight at the D
You force the D's direction from the right, your linemate cuts off the outlet to the left — trap them between you
You back off to the high slot and let your linemate handle the forecheck on their own
Both pull back and reset
Keep moving with the fake and commit hard in that direction
Stop your movement, reset your feet, and regain your gap — don't fully commit
Drop and block the shot
Poke check right away
Hold the puck and fight straight through the pressure — protect it with your body
Move the puck quickly before the pressure arrives — pass to the half-wall or the other side
Shoot it through the pressure no matter the lane — a shot is a shot
Back the puck up toward center ice so you can regroup and enter again
Yes — pinch down to the corner and help your winger win the battle for the puck
No — your winger has possession and your half-wall winger can support. Hold the blue line.
Yes — any time a teammate is battling for the puck you should pinch down
Skate to the net-front instead
Stop and hold the puck to see how the defenders start to set up
Attack the scrambled D immediately — they haven't recovered. Be the second wave.
Pass back to your own D
Skate to the corner
Try to carry the puck out yourself
Stop it for your left defenseman who is coming to get it — get the puck to their forehand
Shoot it the length of the ice and let your forwards chase it
Freeze it
Right in the middle of the net
Slightly cheated to the right — toward the puck carrier's side — while staying square to the puck
Pressed tight to the right post — cover that side completely
Pressed tight to the left post — cover the far-side pass
Stay in the middle of your crease and wait for the puck to appear on the other side
Move with them — push to the right post as they come around and seal it. Don't let them get the post.
Come out past the crease to stop them
Drop into the butterfly right away and slide across behind you
It wastes time
It gets your team closer to the net and creates better scoring chances
It tires out your own forwards
It gives the defense time to reset
Pass to the right winger
Pass to the left winger
Drive the middle lane and shoot — the defenders opened it for you
Pull up and circle back
Stay with your current assignment and trust that someone else will pick the third up
Pick up the late attacker — your winger on that side needs to find their assignment
Call it out loudly so a D on the other side can come over to cover
Ignore the trailer and let your D partner figure the coverage out
Attempt the long stretch pass to the blue line and hope it sneaks through the two defenders
Protect the puck tight to the boards with your body, wait for the angle to open, then make a short chip pass
Try to spin through both defenders with quick footwork and create your own lane out
Shoot the puck hard around the boards and trust a teammate is there on the other side
Say nothing — it's a D problem
Call out loudly to your nearest forward — 'Front! Front!' — so they know to pick up the open player
Skate out of your crease to cover the open attacker in front of the net yourself
Drop into your butterfly early and wait for the pass to come out of the corner
Force a pass across to your left winger even though a defender is on them
Pass to the right winger — they're open and the forechecker has committed to that side
Carry it out yourself
Rim it around the boards
Hold the puck at the half-wall and wait
Thread the seam pass to the middle player — the PK forward cheating toward you opened that
Shoot from the half-wall — any shot on net with a PP advantage is a good one
Pass the puck back to the point and let them try to shoot from up high
Freeze the puck and take the faceoff in your own zone
Pass to the winger coming back — beat the forecheck before they can set up
Rim it hard around the boards past the forecheckers
Shoot it the length of the ice toward the far end
Keep cycling low and wait for the slot to finally clear out for a scoring chance
Pass back to the open point — four defenders below the dots means the point shot is uncontested
Shoot from the corner no matter the angle and hope for a bounce off the goalie
Dump it back in and change lines
Dump the puck deep into the corner and chase it with the clock winding down
Shoot from wherever you are — any shot is better than nothing with 10 seconds left
Skate the puck back out, regroup, and try to set up a clean zone entry again
Hold the puck and protect it
Cheat over toward the open passing option
Set for the shot — your D is taking away the pass, you take the shot
Rush at the puck carrier
Come out past the top of your crease
Stay exactly where you are
Push hard across — you're out of position for the new shot location. Get there fast.
Drop to the butterfly and stay set where you were
Come out of your crease
Win it backward to your D
Win it forward — kick it toward the corner or boards to prevent the PP shot
It doesn't matter where the puck goes as long as you win the faceoff
Tie up the opposing center's stick right after the puck drops
You go — you're the highest forward so the rimmed puck is your responsibility
The near winger goes — they're in better position. You stay high and be the next pass option.
Both forwards chase it hard together so one of you wins the race to the boards
The D jumps up from below the dots to retrieve the rimmed puck
Hold the puck and try to fight through the pressure yourself
Make a quick D-to-D pass to your partner — they're open and you're not
Fire the puck through the traffic
Chip the puck back down into the corner
Pass it across to your teammate as planned
Shoot — the goalie has moved and there's a lane on your side
Pass back the other way
Pull up and regroup
Go for the shorthanded goal — attack the net
Clear the zone and get back — a clean clear is a win on the PK
Pass back to your goalie
Dump it back in
Slow the pace down so your own forwards can save their legs
Keep cycling and creating traffic — tired defensemen make mistakes under sustained
Shoot from every spot on the ice no matter the angle or lane
Change lines right away before the tired defenders can recover their wind
Shoot right away because your coach is yelling at you to shoot
Pass to the better position — the right play is still the right play
Hold the puck and take a second to think about options
Skate toward the bench for a line change
Down in the corner helping your teammate fight
On the right side near the net, ready for a cross-ice pass
At the blue line
Heading back to the bench
Shoot right away
Pass to your open teammate
Slow down and wait
Dump it in the corner
Stay on the weak side in case the shot goes wide immediately
Drive hard to the net — be there for the rebound on the weak side
Hold at the half-wall
Back up to the blue line
